Name: Marie Saulnier Ostéopathe D.O à Audenge
Description:
Bienvenue sur ma page LinkedIn ! Je suis ostéopathe spécialisée, passionnée par l’accompagnement global des patients pour favoriser leur bien-être et leur équilibre corporel.

Mon approche repose sur une vision holistique de la santé, visant à traiter les maux du quotidien tout en prenant en compte les besoins spécifiques de chaque individu. J’interviens auprès des adultes, des femmes enceintes, des enfants et des nourrissons, avec une expertise particulière dans la gestion des douleurs chroniques, des tensions musculaires et des troubles fonctionnels.

Pourquoi l’ostéopathie ?
L’ostéopathie est une thérapie manuelle douce et non invasive qui agit en profondeur pour rétablir l’équilibre naturel du corps. Grâce à des techniques adaptées, elle permet de soulager efficacement les douleurs, de réduire les tensions et de favoriser une meilleure mobilité. Que ce soit pour des douleurs articulaires, des maux de dos, des troubles digestifs, ou encore pour accompagner les changements corporels pendant la grossesse, l’ostéopathie apporte un soulagement durable et améliore la qualité de vie.

Ma philosophie
Chaque patient est unique. C’est pourquoi je m’efforce de créer un espace d’écoute et de confiance, où chacun se sent pleinement pris en charge. Mon objectif est de comprendre et de répondre aux besoins spécifiques de chacun de mes patients en offrant un accompagnement personnalisé.

Ce que vous trouverez ici
Sur cette page, je partage des articles, des conseils pratiques et des informations autour de l’ostéopathie et du bien-être. Mon but est de sensibiliser et d’informer sur les bienfaits de cette approche pour une santé optimale. Vous trouverez également des témoignages, des études de cas et des actualités liées à mon cabinet et aux pratiques de l’ostéopathie.

N’hésitez pas à me suivre pour rester informé(e) sur les dernières nouveautés en matière de soins ostéopathiques et de santé holistique, ou à me contacter pour toute question ou prise de rendez-vous.

Name: Memaxi
Description:
Memaxi assists people who require long-term care and assistance and connects them with their family, friends and professional care providers.

The person receiving assistance sees the daily schedule, notes, pictures, weather information, guestbook entries and more on an easy-to-use, intuitive graphical display.

The family and professional carers add information to the display from afar and communicate both internally and with the person with one-touch video calling. Memaxi makes it easy to share tasks and responsibilities. Audio and visual prompts remind users of upcoming events, assistance needed and tasks to be completed.

Our users are the elderly, people with Alzheimer’s disease, patients under long-term care, people with physical or mental disabilities as well as families who simply need something to run the household.  That is why we have made Memaxi highly customisable to fit individual needs and preferences. 

If you are a person who wants a tighter control of today’s events, someone who needs outside help in leading an organised life or if you receive long-term, organised care then Memaxi is your key. Memaxi eases and enriches your daily routine, improving the overall quality of life at the same time.

Memaxi is available both for individuals who need a tighter control of the daily schedule and organisations such as care homes, assisted living facilities, home service providers, day centres and rehabilitation clinics providing assistance to multiple persons.

Name: Brain of Dietitian Pty Ltd
Description:
We are a team of Accredited Practising Dietitians specialising in nutrition therapy and meal preparation services for NDIS participants. 
With a strong foundation in dietetic practice, our goal is to harness the power of nutrition to enhance the well-being and independence of individuals with diverse needs. 

We provide top quality services, including: 

 Nutrition Consultations
Nutrition consultations are conducted within the comfort of our clients homes. We delve into anthropometry, biochemistry, medical and family history, medications, and dietary habits which forms the basis of nutrition education, personalised treatment plans, and tailored resources.

 Tailored Programs
Our in-home cooking and educational programs involve our dietitian's working closely with participants for 8, 10, or 12 weeks. Together, we aim to improve culinary skills and nutritional knowledge. Programs are highly tailored to participant preferences and specific needs.

 Prepped Meal Service
Our prepped meal service aims to provide convenient and nutritious meals that support the needs of our participants. We involve participants in menu creation, offering choice and control of their menu. Meals are made fresh with responsibly sourced ingredients, delivered in eco-friendly containers.

At Brain of a Dietitian Pty Ltd, we are committed to delivering personalised, evidence-based dietary strategies that empower our participants and their support team.

Name: Cassena Care At Home
Description:
Cassena Care at Home provides comprehensive in-home care that cares for the whole patient — building confidence, diminishing readmissions, and driving the best health outcomes.

Our in-home teams quickly understand patient needs and holistically manage ongoing recoveries. Our leadership assures insightful diagnosis and development of individualized care plans. Our network of clinicians, outpatient partners and therapists support ongoing patient health. And our relationships with facility teams drive seamless patient journeys.

We are dedicated to delivering the highest quality nursing care in the region. Patients are assigned a registered nurse as a primary care manager who is responsible for overseeing all care needs. Our nurse will collaborate with patients, families, and doctors to develop personalized care plans and coordinate any other home based services.

Our therapists apply functional-based techniques to treat injury and disease with an emphasis on improving functional abilities. After hospitalization, illness or surgery, our rehab care team creates individualized care plans to realize the best health outcomes.

Our social workers collaborate with patients and their families to holistically understand health care needs and match those needs with available programs and services. They are highly skilled in addressing concerns that arise in both home environments and social settings that are essential to improving patient and family quality of life.

Home Health aids are assigned to provide essential personal care, including bathing, dressing, and meal preparation. Additional services may be available to help patients and their families with the activities of daily living.

Name: Kate's Home Nursing
Description:
Kate’s Home Nursing provides professional, compassionate, specialist nursing ‘hospice at home’ care, at a time that can often be an emotional and frightening time for patients and families alike.

Our bank of experienced Registered Nurses provide expert nursing care for people in the last stage of illness who wish to die at home. We seek to ensure the best possible pain relief and symptom control. We also provide much-needed respite for the patient’s family or carer.

There is no set pattern of care. Our nursing is arranged according to need from a few hours of respite for a patient’s family to all night nursing, with particular emphasis on effective pain relief and symptom control.

Name: Homewatch CareGivers of Sparks-North Reno
Description:
Caring for your loved one can be a challenging task, but at Homewatch CareGivers of Sparks-North Reno, we make it easier for you. Our highly trained staff is dedicated to the well-being of your loved one. We understand how frustrating it can be to find the right caregiver, so we are here to help.

At the heart of our business is client satisfaction. We are committed to providing person-directed care that is flexible and tailored to your needs. Whether you need care for just an hour or 24 hours a day, seven days a week, we are here to help. Our ultimate goal is to keep you or your loved one independent, enjoying the daily living and activities you are used to.

We start by conducting an in-home assessment with you and your family member to develop a personalized Care Plan that grows with you and changes as your needs change. Our home care services include companion care, personal care, and quality-of-life care. We cater to seniors, military veterans, new mothers, children, surgery patients, and anyone who needs assistance to remain in the comfort of their own homes. We are a home health agency for people of all ages.

Our caregivers are professionals passionate about providing personalized care for your family. They undergo background checks, routine drug testing, and enrolment in our proprietary Homewatch University. Our caregivers receive ongoing training to maintain our high quality of care. Through personalized caregiver assessments, we can match the most compatible caregiver to the person needing care.

Our goal is to provide care for those in need in the comfort of their own homes with compassion, dignity, and purpose.

Name: SeenCare
Description:
As Melbourne’s first doctor and nurse led NDIS provider, SeenCare is on a mission to transform the way care is delivered to NDIS participants with high medical needs.

With over 54 years of combined experience in the disability and healthcare sectors, we understand the challenges people living with co-existing disability and medical needs face in accessing high quality community care. 

Our multi-disciplinary team includes specialist disability support workers, home nurses and bulk-billing GPs. This allows us to deliver a truly unique model of integrated care that is specially tailored to help participants with complex needs thrive in the community.

In addition to offering the full range of standard NDIS services, we can also provide:

- 24/7 clinical support
- Supported independent living (SIL)
- STA, MTA and supports for SDA residents
- Complex hospital discharge support
- Chronic disease management
- Healthcare coordination
- Tracheostomy care
- Catheter & stoma support
- Overnight care
- High-risk medication management
- Complex bowel care

To find our more or to make a referral, please visit the website or email us at info@seencare.com.au

Name: Highland Home Carers
Description:
Whatever you need to support you in your own home we can help. We offer a wide range of services to meet your personal care needs from first thing in the morning to last thing at night. We can help you with getting out of bed, going to the toilet, getting washed and dressed. We can make your breakfast and make sure you get your medication.

Highland Home Carers was founded in 1994. Since then we have grown to become one of the major independent providers of home care and support services in Scotland. We currently employ over 250 staff and have a financial turnover of over £5 million. Our company has been owned by our employees since 2004. The business is overseen by a Board which is made up of Executive Directors, Employee Elected Directors and Non Executive Directors.

Home Care
We can make sure lunch is ready for you to eat later in the day or we can pop in and prepare it with you. In the evening we can help with your meal and any medication and at the end of the day help you to bed and make sure the house is secure.

Support Work
Whatever you need to support you in your own home we can help. We offer a wide range of services to meet your personal care needs from first thing in the morning to last thing at night. We can help you with getting out of bed, going to the toilet, getting washed and dressed. We can make your breakfast and make sure you get your medication.

Complex Care
No matter how complex your needs we can help. We offer specialised supported living services on a one to one 24 hour basis and have enabled many people over the years to stay in their own homes.
We also offer specialised dementia care and have highly trained staff with years’ of experience in caring for people with dementia.

Care Academy/SVQ Training Centre
We focus on providing thorough, high-quality training programmes to individuals pursuing a career in social care, and to organisations looking to train new staff or keep their existing workforce up to date with the required skills.

Name: Assisting Hands Home Care - Serving Rockford & Surrounding Areas
Description:
Assisting Hands Home Care grew out of a desire to provide seniors, and others needing non-medical assistance at home, with the option that most people prefer – to remain independent, safe and comfortable in their own homes. Assisting Hands is a home care franchise company that emphasizes exceptional customer service and highly personalized in-home care to meet the needs of our clients.

The founder of Assisting Hands Home Care is Dr. Gail Silverstein, who has more than 25 years of experience coordinating and leading health care programs in both the public and private sector. It was Gail’s understanding of the health care industry, along with the personal experience of trying to find assistance for her father at home, that led her to realize there was a serious gap in services. Out of this need, Assisting Hands was launched – to provide a better alternative for the elderly, disabled and others needing a little extra support and assistance to stay home!

OUR CORE PURPOSE
To provide exceptional home care that enriches the lives of our clients and provides peace of mind for their families.

OUR MISSION STATEMENT
To offer our clients personalized non-medical support services in their homes, assisting the elderly, disabled and others needing assistance to maintain quality of life. Our services support the individual’s choices and preferences in order to maintain their dignity and independence. We assist clients in a spirit of concern for their welfare, gaining satisfaction and a sense of pride for the value that we add to their lives.

Name: Inkit Care
Description:
Inkit bridges care and innovation, combining practical insights with advanced technology to manage children's records, care plans, and behavioral progress.
Inkit is an innovative software solution designed to support UK-based residential homes that care for vulnerable children under the age of 18. 

Our platform simplifies the process of tracking and recording children’s behaviours, generating detailed reports that help inform personalised care plans. By integrating technology into the day-to-day operations, Inkit ensures that every child receives the attention and support they need to thrive.

Inkit allows caregivers and support workers to monitor real-time progress and respond quickly to any changes in behaviour or needs. This ensures a consistent, nurturing environment for every child, making it easier for care providers to meet the highest standards of children care.

Inkit is revolutionising the way care homes operate, combining cutting-edge technology with compassionate care to improve the lives of children across the UK.
